items.summary: SEATTLE — U.S. Department of Labor (DOL) officials came to the annual conference of the American Association of Community Colleges (AACC) this weekend mainly to listen, with a particular interest in learning about innovations and... items.title: Looking for a College Scholarship on Social Media Sites? Buyer, Beware.
items.summary: Students say they trust their peers’ advice more than financial advisers’, a new survey found. But experts say students need to do their own research.

items.title: Renovation backlog for college facilities hits new highs
items.summary: The dollar value of deferred capital projects grew by 8% last year, according to the latest state of higher ed facilities report from Gordian.

items.title: Dozens more colleges get delay in submitting race and sex admissions data
items.summary: A federal judge extended the deadline for several private colleges and higher education groups to submit data to the U.S. Department of Education.

items.title: When families are informed, students stay enrolled
items.summary: Family access to critical student data is linked to higher retention and persistence.

items.title: Food Insecurity Higher for Working, Caregiving Students
items.summary: Food Insecurity Higher for Working, Caregiving Students Joshua.Bay Mon, 04/13/2026 - 03:00 AM A new analysis from the Institute for Higher Education Policy found that food insecurity was associated with lower persistence among older, working and caregiving college students. Byline(s) Joshua Bay

items.title: Per-student state funding for higher ed dips for first time in years
items.summary: Enrollment gains at public colleges surpassed increases in state and local support in the 2025 fiscal year, according to an annual report.

items.title: Building AI Initiatives with Bottom-Up Champions
items.summary: Driven by a bottom-up partnership between the Faculty Center for Teaching and Learning and the Division of Digital Learning, the University of Central Florida established an evolving campus infrastructure of policies, training, and a national conference to guide the ethical and effective integration of generative AI into teaching and learning.

items.title: The power of partnerships
items.summary: A decade ago, Marion Technical College did not have the means to pursue meaningful grants. The two-year institution, which shares a campus with Ohio State University, lacked a dedicated grant office as well as a... The post The power of partnerships first appeared on Community College Daily .

items.title: Roll call on community college research
items.summary: The Council for the Study of Community Colleges — an affiliated council of the American Association of Community Colleges (AACC) — just hosted its 67th annual conference. Top of mind for researchers and leaders in... The post Roll call on community college research first appeared on Community College Daily .

items.title: COVID remote learning put drain on college enrollment
items.summary: The percentages of students completing the FAFSA, taking the ACT or signing up for the first year of college fell in 2020-21, NBER data shows.

items.title: Kansas governor signs bill to curb race-related instruction at public colleges
items.summary: The legislation will prohibit public institutions from requiring “DEI-CRT” courses, which will be up to the Kansas Board of Regents to define.

items.title: Advancing AI Literacy: A Faculty Course Refresh Institute at Indiana Wesleyan University
items.summary: Redesigning learning experiences, not just adding tools, can build faculty confidence and develop students’ critical, ethical, and applied AI literacy.

items.title: The Human Side of Data at Any Scale
items.summary: Sophie and Jenay talk with Nadeem Syed of Western Governor's University and Shannon Shank of California Institute of the Arts about how institutions of any size can consider foundational approaches to data for informed decision-making.

items.title: Brown University Keeps Police Chief Who Took Over After Shooting
items.summary: Hugh T. Clements Jr. was a fixture of the Providence Police Department before becoming Brown’s interim chief after the fatal attack in December.

items.title: When preparation meets opportunity

items.title: A deep dive into poverty

That’s... The post A deep dive into poverty first appeared on Community College Daily .

items.title: Snapshots: Scenes in Seattle
items.summary: A few candid photos from AACC 2026 this week in Seattle. (Photos: Adam Auel/AACC) The post Snapshots: Scenes in Seattle first appeared on Community College Daily .

items.summary: SEATTLE — The nation’s leading association for community colleges is embarking on a reset that will guide its work over the next decade. On Saturday night at the opening plenary of the American Association of... The post AACC’s bold new vision first appeared on Community College Daily .

items.title: Observations about the House’s WIOA bill
items.summary: Timing is everything. Never has the A Stronger Workforce for America Act of 2026 been more relevant to community colleges. top_signals.title: MA Budget: Community College SUCCESS Fund (7100-4002)
top_signals.why_it_matters: Anchor page for tracking SUCCESS language, funding, and wraparound-support intent through the budget process.
top_signals.summary_for_brief: Anchor page for tracking SUCCESS language, funding, and wraparound-support intent through the budget process.
top_signals.core_story: The core story here is about whether public policy is backing institutional expectations with real dollars and implementation capacity.
top_signals.state_relevance: For Massachusetts, the value of this story is comparative. It raises the question of whether community colleges are being funded and supported at a level that matches the expectations being placed on them.
comparison_points: This can be framed as an investment comparison: what level of funding is another state prepared to put behind reform, and how does that compare with current commitments in Massachusetts?
top_signals.recommended_angle: Frame this as an investment-gap question for Massachusetts: are leaders expecting community colleges to do system-changing work without system-level funding?
top_signals.claim: Massachusetts is increasingly treating advising, transfer support, and wraparound services as core completion infrastructure rather than optional supports.
top_signals.implication: The key question is no longer whether these supports matter, but whether colleges and the state can fund and scale them effectively.

top_signals.title: MassTransfer
top_signals.why_it_matters: Anchor page for Massachusetts transfer policy, useful when comparing other states’ transfer, articulation, and pathway reforms.
top_signals.summary_for_brief: Anchor page for Massachusetts transfer policy, useful when comparing other states’ transfer, articulation, and pathway reforms.
top_signals.core_story: The core story is whether policy commitments are being matched by the student-support infrastructure needed to improve persistence and completion.
top_signals.state_relevance: For Massachusetts, the value of this story is comparative. It raises the question of whether community colleges are being funded and supported at a level that matches the expectations being placed on them.
comparison_points: This story may offer a useful comparison point for policy and practice in Massachusetts, especially if leaders are asking whether ambition is matched by implementation capacity.
top_signals.recommended_angle: Use this as a policy comparison story that helps leaders in Massachusetts think about investment, implementation, and institutional capacity.
top_signals.claim: Advising is emerging as a structural lever for completion, transfer, and workforce success—not just a student-services function.
top_signals.implication: Colleges that treat advising as institutional infrastructure will be better positioned than those relying on fragmented or purely reactive models.

top_signals.recommended_angle: Use this as a policy comparison story that helps leaders in Massachusetts think about investment, implementation, and institutional capacity.
top_signals.claim: Federal policy is accelerating the shift toward short-term, workforce-aligned programs as a central community college strategy.
top_signals.implication: Community colleges will need stronger advising, employer alignment, and outcomes reporting if they want to benefit from the next phase of federal support.

linkedin_drafts.title: Massachusetts policy watch: a comparison worth making
linkedin_drafts.text:
One higher-ed development worth watching this week is MA Budget: Community College SUCCESS Fund (7100-4002) (https://budget.digital.mass.gov/govbudget/fy27/appropriations/education/higher-education/71004002/).

Anchor page for tracking SUCCESS language, funding, and wraparound-support intent through the budget process.

What stands out to me is that The core story here is about whether public policy is backing institutional expectations with real dollars and implementation capacity.

For Massachusetts, the key question is whether community colleges are being given the investment, staffing, and policy infrastructure needed to do similar work at scale.

Why this matters: For Massachusetts, the value of this story is comparative. It raises the question of whether community colleges are being funded and supported at a level that matches the expectations being placed on them. Anchor page for tracking SUCCESS language, funding, and wraparound-support intent through the budget process.

That is where the real policy conversation should begin.

#Massachusetts #CommunityColleges #StudentSuccess #HigherEdPolicy #AcademicAdvising

linkedin_drafts.title: Massachusetts student success infrastructure
linkedin_drafts.text:
Massachusetts is increasingly treating advising, transfer support, and wraparound services as core completion infrastructure rather than optional supports.

A good example is MA Budget: Community College SUCCESS Fund (7100-4002) (https://budget.digital.mass.gov/govbudget/fy27/appropriations/education/higher-education/71004002/). Anchor page for tracking SUCCESS language, funding, and wraparound-support intent through the budget process.

What matters for community colleges is this: The key question is no longer whether these supports matter, but whether colleges and the state can fund and scale them effectively.

That is where the real strategic question sits right now—not just what changed, but how institutions respond.

#Massachusetts #CommunityColleges #StudentSuccess #AcademicAdvising #HigherEdPolicy

linkedin_drafts.title: Advising and student support
linkedin_drafts.text:
Advising is emerging as a structural lever for completion, transfer, and workforce success—not just a student-services function.

A good example is MassTransfer (https://www.mass.edu/masstransfer/). Anchor page for Massachusetts transfer policy, useful when comparing other states’ transfer, articulation, and pathway reforms.

What matters for community colleges is this: Colleges that treat advising as institutional infrastructure will be better positioned than those relying on fragmented or purely reactive models.

That is where the real strategic question sits right now—not just what changed, but how institutions respond.

#HigherEdPolicy #CommunityColleges #StudentSuccess #AcademicAdvising #EdTech

linkedin_drafts.title: Workforce Pell and short-term pathways
linkedin_drafts.text:
Federal policy is accelerating the shift toward short-term, workforce-aligned programs as a central community college strategy.

A good example is Metallica foundation adds $10M to workforce initiative (https://www.ccdaily.com/2026/04/metallica-foundation-adds-10m-to-workforce-initiative/?utm_source=rss&utm_medium=rss&utm_campaign=metallica-foundation-adds-10m-to-workforce-initiative). SEATTLE — The foundation of the rock band Metallica plans to invest $10 million over the next three years in its successful workforce development initiative, which has supported 13,000 community college students since it started... The post Metallica foundation adds $10M to workforce initiative first appeared on Community College Daily .

What matters for community colleges is this: Community colleges will need stronger advising, employer alignment, and outcomes reporting if they want to benefit from the next phase of federal support.

That is where the real strategic question sits right now—not just what changed, but how institutions respond.

#HigherEdPolicy #CommunityColleges #StudentSuccess #AcademicAdvising #EdTech
